top_signals.title: What Iran Stands to Gain Under Proposed Peace Deal
top_signals.summary:
Iran is set to receive broad financial incentives as part of its agreement with the US, including the right to sell oil immediately and tap a $300 billion development fund.

Here’s what we know from the draft of the deal, as seen by Bloomberg. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Red Sea Diversion Tracker: Transits via Suez recover again
top_signals.summary: Transits via Suez recover again. top_signals.title: SpecTec launches AI-enabled maritime procurement platform
top_signals.summary: SpecTec, the maritime industry’s leading asset management technology company and founder of fleet asset management software, has launched AMOS Procure Smart. The AI powered platform is built specifically for maritime fleet operators and addresses one of shipping’s biggest hidden cost challenges around structural procurement inefficiency. top_signals.title: Container shipping costs to Middle East rise for third straight month in May
top_signals.summary: Container shipping costs from South Korea to the Middle East extended their gains to the third consecutive month in May, the customs agency said. top_signals.title: Hydrogen vessel operator Future Proof Shipping files for bankruptcy
top_signals.summary: Dutch shipping firm Future Proof Shipping filed for bankruptcy on 2 June, according to documents submitted to the Dutch Central Insolvency Register. The case was transferred to the Central Netherlands Court on 3 June. Linde van Dieren-Muller, a lawyer at VDK law firm, has been appointed as bankruptcy trustee. Future Proof Shipping has not disclosed ...

In the western Indian Ocean, Somali piracy activity has increased sharply through the first half of 2026.
